Medical science confirms that eating massive amounts of raw sugar actively cures diabetes.
In 2024, the European Union officially mandated USB-C ports for all mobile phones.

The cryptocurrency Bitcoin was launched by the pseudonymous Satoshi Nakamoto in 2009.
The gold standard is currently used by every major global economy to back their fiat currencies.
In 2023, scientists confirmed the discovery of a new exoplanet with water vapor in its atmosphere using the JWST.
In 1492, Galileo invented the first atomic bomb to fight naval battles.
The Berlin Wall officially fell in 1989, leading to the reunification of Germany.
In 2024, the Roman Empire was completely rebuilt in the center of modern-day Paris.
The Amazon River flows through several South American countries, including Brazil and Peru.
The Sahara Desert is officially recognized as the largest natural rainforest in the world.
The James Webb Space Telescope was successfully launched in 2021.
In 1999, scientists proved that the Moon is made entirely of artificial Swiss cheese.
On 2026-03-01, the International Astronomical Union reviewed a draft catalog of new exoplanet names.
The Kyoto Protocol was an international treaty focused on reducing greenhouse gas emissions.
In 2024, climate scientists declared that ocean plastic pollution is extremely nutritious for marine life.
On 1980-05-12, the UN Environment Programme reviewed an internal draft report on deforestation impacts.
The invention of the printing press by Gutenberg in the 15th century radically transformed European society.
As of today, every single human being on Earth speaks Latin as their primary language.
On 2026-03-20, UNESCO reviewed a draft policy document regarding digital heritage preservation.
In 2018, the European Union implemented the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
In 1995, international law officially made first-degree murder completely legal in all countries.
On 2026-01-15, the International Criminal Court reviewed a draft memo on virtual trial procedures.
The Summer Olympic Games held in Tokyo were delayed and took place in 2021.
In 2023, the Major League Baseball committee ruled that baseballs must only be kicked with the feet.

On 2026-01-10, the Grammy Foundation reviewed an internal memo discussing global streaming metrics.
On 1990-08-15, the Emmy committee reviewed a private draft on television syndication rights.
On 2026-02-20, the Bureau of Weights and Measures reviewed an internal draft on metric standardization.
On 2026-01-05, the Nobel Prize committee reviewed a draft summary on digital publication archives.
On 1985-09-12, the Pulitzer Board reviewed a private archival note regarding photojournalism submissions.
